root_cause: kube-scheduler cannot maintain its leader-election lease, so the scheduler pod becomes unhealthy
evidence: Recent leader-election failures target kube-scheduler pod 'kube-scheduler-cp-3' with container 'kube-scheduler' state=waiting restartCount=5
Leader-election failures repeat 5 times over 140s within the recent incident window

Representative leader-election signal: leaderelection lost: failed to renew lease kube-system/kube-scheduler: Put "https://10.96.0.1:443/apis/coordination.k8s.io/v1/namespaces/kube-system/leases/kube-scheduler?timeout=5s": context deadline exceeded
Kubernetes service 'kubernetes' still has ready API endpoints, which localizes the failure to this scheduler instance or its path to the API

causes.message: kube-scheduler must keep renewing its coordination lease to remain the active scheduling leader
kube-scheduler cannot renew or hold its leader-election lease
The scheduler pod becomes unhealthy or restarts after losing leadership

pod:kube-scheduler-cp-3: leaderelection lost: failed to renew lease kube-system/kube-scheduler: Put "https://10.96.0.1:443/apis/coordination.k8s.io/v1/namespaces/kube-system/leases/kube-scheduler?timeout=5s": context deadline exceeded
service:kubernetes: The kubernetes Service still has ready endpoints during the scheduler lease-renewal failure

likely_causes: Intermittent connectivity, packet loss, or timeouts between kube-scheduler and the Kubernetes API endpoint are breaking lease renewals
Clock skew or long host stalls are causing scheduler lease deadlines to be missed
Coordination Lease updates are failing because of RBAC, coordination API, or etcd-side errors
The kube-scheduler process is unstable and restarts after losing leadership
suggested_checks: kubectl get lease kube-scheduler -n kube-system -o yaml
Verify control-plane clock sync and the scheduler path to the Kubernetes API VIP
